---
title: Collections
---

When you render a `_calepin.toml` manifest, *Calepin* renders a collection of `.qmd` files into a structured website with navigation, sidebar, search, dark mode, and a consistent layout.


# Quick start

1. Create a `_calepin.toml` in your project root:

```toml
[meta]
title = "My Site"

[site]
target = "website"
content = [
  "index.qmd",
  "about.qmd",
  ["Guide",
    "guide/intro.qmd",
    "guide/advanced.qmd",
  ],
]
```

2. Build the collection:

```
calepin render _calepin.toml
```

3. Preview locally:

```
calepin preview _calepin.toml
```

The collection is written to `_site/` by default (or the `output` field if specified).


# Configuration

The `_calepin.toml` file configures your collection. You can also name it `calepin.toml`.

## Output

```toml
output = "docs"    # output directory (default: inferred from target)
```

## Collection metadata

```toml
[meta]
title = "My Site"
subtitle = "A description"
url = "https://example.com"
csl = "chicago-author-date"
highlight = {light = "github", dark = "nord"}
```

## Collection section

```toml
[site]
target = "website"
favicon = "assets/favicon.svg"
logo = "assets/logo.svg"
logo-dark = "assets/logo-dark.svg"
```

## Content tree

The `content` field defines the sidebar navigation. It accepts flat entries, glob patterns, and nested sections (using arrays where the first element is the section label):

```toml
[site]
content = [
  "index.qmd",
  ["Guide",
    "guide/intro.qmd",
    "guide/advanced.qmd",
    ["Subsection",
      "guide/sub/a.qmd",
    ],
  ],
  ["Blog", "posts/*.qmd"],    # glob pattern
]
content_standalone = [         # rendered but not in navigation
  "404.qmd",
]
```

Document titles are extracted from YAML front matter.

## Variables

The `[var]` section provides collection-wide variables accessible in templates and document bodies:

```toml
[var]
primary = "#0066cc"
font = "Inter"
navbar_search = true
sidebar_collapse_level = 0

[[var.navbar_right]]
icon = "github"
href = "https://github.com/user/repo"
```

Available icons: `github`, `twitter`, `x`, `bluesky`, `mastodon`, `linkedin`, `orcid`, `rss`, `email`, `globe`, `youtube`.

## Custom targets

Override built-in targets or define new ones:

```toml
[targets.latex]
base = "latex"
template = "calepin"
extension = "tex"
fig-extension = "pdf"
[targets.latex.compile]
command = "tectonic {input}"
extension = "pdf"
```


# Listings

A document can display a listing of other documents (for blogs, news, etc.):

```yaml
---
title: Blog
listing:
  contents: "posts/*.qmd"
  type: default
  sort: "date desc"
---
```

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `contents` | Glob pattern for documents to list |
| `type` | Layout: `default` (cards), `table`, or `grid` |
| `sort` | Sort order: `"date desc"`, `"title asc"`, etc. |


# Customizing collection templates

Collection templates control the HTML structure of every document. To override any template, create files in `templates/website/` at your project root.

## Built-in templates

| Template | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `base.html` | Root layout: head, scripts, overall structure |
| `page.html` | Standard document (extends `base.html`) |
| `listing.html` | Listing document with card/table/grid (extends `base.html`) |
| `navbar.html` | Top navigation bar |
| `sidebar_left.html` | Left sidebar with document tree |
| `sidebar_right.html` | Right sidebar with table of contents |
| `search.html` | Search overlay modal |

## Template variables

Collection templates receive two main context objects:

### collection

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{ collection.title }}`{% endraw %} | Collection title |
| {% raw %}`{{ collection.subtitle }}`{% endraw %} | Collection subtitle |
| {% raw %}`{{ collection.url }}`{% endraw %} | Collection URL |
| {% raw %}`{{ collection.favicon }}`{% endraw %} | Favicon path |
| {% raw %}`{{ collection.logo }}`{% endraw %} | Logo path |
| {% raw %}`{{ collection.logo_dark }}`{% endraw %} | Dark mode logo path |
| {% raw %}`{{ collection.pages }}`{% endraw %} | Navigation tree (with active document marked) |
| {% raw %}`{{ collection.dark_mode }}`{% endraw %} | Whether dark mode is enabled |
| {% raw %}`{{ collection.math_block }}`{% endraw %} | Math library script tags |

### document

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{ document.title }}`{% endraw %} | Document title |
| {% raw %}`{{ document.subtitle }}`{% endraw %} | Document subtitle |
| {% raw %}`{{ document.date }}`{% endraw %} | Document date |
| {% raw %}`{{ document.body }}`{% endraw %} | Rendered HTML content |
| {% raw %}`{{ document.toc }}`{% endraw %} | Table of contents HTML |
| {% raw %}`{{ document.url }}`{% endraw %} | Document URL path |
| {% raw %}`{{ document.source_url }}`{% endraw %} | Path to `.qmd` source file |
| {% raw %}`{{ document.listing }}`{% endraw %} | List of listing items (for listing documents) |
| {% raw %}`{{ document.breadcrumbs }}`{% endraw %} | Breadcrumb trail |
| {% raw %}`{{ document.prev }}`{% endraw %} | Previous document link (`text`, `href`) |
| {% raw %}`{{ document.next }}`{% endraw %} | Next document link (`text`, `href`) |

### Functions

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| {% raw %}`{{ icon(name="github") }}`{% endraw %} | Insert an SVG icon by name |

## Example: custom document template

Create `templates/website/page.html` to override the default document layout:

```html
{% extends "base.html" %}
{% block content %}
<article>
  <h1>{{ document.title }}</h1>
  {% if document.date %}<time>{{ document.date }}</time>{% endif %}
  {{ document.body }}
</article>
{% endblock %}

{% block page_nav %}
<nav class="page-nav">
  {% if document.prev %}<a href="{{ document.prev.href }}">Previous</a>{% endif %}
  {% if document.next %}<a href="{{ document.next.href }}">Next</a>{% endif %}
</nav>
{% endblock %}
```

The base template provides blocks you can override: `head_extra`, `content`, `page_nav`, `footer`.


# CLI

```
calepin render _calepin.toml             # Build collection
calepin render _calepin.toml -o docs     # Build to docs/
calepin render _calepin.toml --clean     # Clean output before building
calepin preview _calepin.toml            # Build and serve locally
calepin preview _calepin.toml -p 4000    # Custom port
```
